text: Fill in the blanks on enzyme structure, kinetics, inhibition, and clinical applications.
questions: The protein part of an enzyme without its cofactor is called the *apoenzyme/Apoenzyme*, while the complete catalytically active enzyme with its cofactor is called the *holoenzyme/Holoenzyme*.
In Michaelis-Menten kinetics, the *Km/Michaelis constant* represents the substrate concentration at which the reaction velocity is *half/50%/one-half* of Vmax.
A *competitive/Competitive* inhibitor binds to the *active site/Active site* of the enzyme and increases the apparent Km without changing *Vmax/maximum velocity*.
The six classes of enzymes according to IUBMB are oxidoreductases, transferases, *hydrolases/Hydrolases*, *lyases/Lyases*, isomerases, and *ligases/Ligases*.
Serum *troponin/Troponin/troponin I* is the most specific cardiac enzyme marker used to diagnose *myocardial infarction/MI/heart attack*.
